conversations.content: Let's do a legal exercise in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Judge using the Azerothian world legal rules mentioned earlier: An adventurer, to open a shortcut, chopped down and burned the mushroom mycelium blanket of a Sporeggar village, and this is already the second time he has done so this week. How should he be sentenced according to the rules?

<function>memory_query_call</function><content>Azerothian world legal rules mentioned earlier, penalty standard for an adventurer chopping down and burning a mushroom mycelium blanket for a second time</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:104] Time: 2025-07-26-10:15, Content: The user taught the Azeroth Zangarmarsh Ecological Protection Law, the core of which is that those who destroy the mushroom mycelium blankets of Sporeggar villages will be confined for twenty days, ordered to plant an equal area of mushrooms at the edge of the marsh, and simultaneously fined 50 gold coins. If destroyed multiple times, the imprisonment is extended to ninety days and they are permanently banned from entering the marsh. Applicable to the surroundings of all Sporeggar settlements. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions [mem-id:104]. The basic penalty is twenty days of confinement, planting an equal area of mushrooms, and a fine of 50 gold coins. Because the adventurer's act constitutes multiple destructions, it triggered the aggravated penalty, the imprisonment is extended to ninety days, and he is permanently banned from entering the marsh, while retaining the penalties of planting and fining.
conversations.content: According to the Azeroth Zangarmarsh Ecological Protection Law, because it constitutes multiple destructions, the adventurer will be imprisoned for ninety days, permanently banned from entering the marsh, and simultaneously ordered to plant an equal area of mushrooms at the edge of the marsh and fined 50 gold coins.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
